review_comment: It would be better to set a flag in this case and print this log message after `UART_EXIT_CRITICAL` and before `break;` in case the flag is set. Entering and exiting critical sections is rather expensive compared to setting and testing a flag
Why do you need to care about performance in an unlikely error path?
You are right about performance, in fact, I meant that we would prefer to have both variable set atomically, in order to avoid being pre-empted in-between
